title: strychnos colombiensis
intro: Strychnos colombiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae, native to Colombia and parts of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural source of strychnine, a potent alkaloid with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Strychnos colombiensis include its potential use as a pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, as well as its possible application in the treatment of various diseases. Herbal preparations of Strychnos colombiensis are typically made by drying and processing the plant's roots and seeds, which are then used to create teas, infusions, and other medicinal products.
